records.title: Enhance documentation for AKS private cluster FQDN
records.summary: When you create an AKS private cluster by using --enable-private-cluster, AKS deploys the Kubernetes API server through a private endpoint inside the virtual network. By default, AKS might still create a public DNS name. Use the following guidance.

records.title: Apply suggestions from code review
records.summary: If you're unfamiliar with VPA, use the following deployment pattern during application development to identify each application's unique resource utilization characteristics, test VPA to verify it's functioning properly. 1. Establish observability first by collecting actual resource utilization telemetry over a given period of time. This telemetry helps you understand the behavior and any signs of issues from container and pod resources.
records.change_summary: If you're unfamiliar with VPA, use the following deployment pattern during application development to identify each application's unique resource utilization characteristics, test VPA to verify it's functioning properly. 1. Establish observability first by collecting actual resource utilization telemetry over a given period of time. This telemetry helps you understand the behavior and any signs of issues from container and pod resources.

records.title: Adding AKS Automatic
records.summary: Kubernetes Event-driven Autoscaling (KEDA) is a single-purpose and lightweight component that makes application autoscaling simple. It's a Cloud Native Computing Foundation (CNCF) Graduate project. KEDA uses event-driven. For most production workloads, AKS Automatic is the recommended default AKS experience. AKS Automatic is production ready by default and includes KEDA preconfigured on the cluster. If you use AKS Standard, you can enable.
records.change_summary: Kubernetes Event-driven Autoscaling (KEDA) is a single-purpose and lightweight component that makes application autoscaling simple. It's a Cloud Native Computing Foundation (CNCF) Graduate project. KEDA uses event-driven. For most production workloads, AKS Automatic is the recommended default AKS experience. AKS Automatic is production ready by default and includes KEDA preconfigured on the cluster. If you use AKS Standard, you can enable.
